dialogueChunks.intro: It's done, {player_name}. The seat is mine, and {location} will be better for it.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The council recognized my claim this morning. {target_npc} conceded after they saw the evidence and the support we'd gathered. The people aren't entirely happy: some think I pushed too hard, others think I didn't push hard enough. But the {enemy_type} threat is being addressed, the supply lines are open, and for the first time in months, decisions are being made instead of debated endlessly.
dialogueChunks.outro: You helped me earn this. Not just take it: earn it. Here's what I promised, and know that when you return to {location}, you'll always have an ally in power.

playerResponses.text: Use the power wisely. That's all I ask.
An ally in power is worth more than gold. I'll hold you to that.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I won't pretend the victory was clean. But it's mine.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The opposition crumbled after you dealt with their mercenaries and I exposed their backers. {target_npc} left {location} rather than face the assembly. Some call me ruthless, and maybe they're right. But the workshops are running, the defenses are strengthened, and the {enemy_type} haven't breached our perimeter since I took charge. The people who called me a tyrant last week are sleeping safely because of the choices I made.
dialogueChunks.outro: History remembers results, not methods. Here's your share of the results.

playerResponses.text: Results that protect people are results I can live with.
Just remember that fear is a poor foundation for loyalty.

dialogueChunks.intro: I pushed too far, {player_name}. I know that now.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: I got what I wanted: the seat, the authority, the control. And it lasted three days before {target_npc} rallied enough support to force a shared council. I'm still at the table, but I'm not the only voice anymore. Part of me is furious. Another part, the part I don't let many people see, is relieved. Leading alone was exhausting in ways I didn't expect. The {enemy_type} don't care about politics, and neither do hungry children.
dialogueChunks.outro: I learned something about ambition: having it isn't the sin. Letting it consume you is. Take this, and know I'm grateful for your honesty, even when it wasn't what I wanted to hear.

playerResponses.text: Shared power is still power. And it's more sustainable.
The fact that you can admit it means you'll be a better leader for it.
